note: P-tabs2 (M5, parity-checklist §B — third-pass rulings 2026-07-03/3) — the remaining §B tab migrations land on the P-panel dock: a SLICE tab (neighbourhood .mmd + copy) and a STYLE tab (appearance), and the tab strip becomes TWO STACKED ROWS (row 1: reveal · io · mermaid; row 2: slice · style) so future tabs never produce one over-long row (row assignment ruled a free decision). nav is CLOSED by ruling — unfold's browse search is the replacement, the navigator dies with legacy; no change in this plan touches it. The slice port keeps ONE serialisation path: SliceApi gains sliceFor(ids) (the files.loadMmdText precedent — the legacy pane and the unfold tab are two triggers of the same proven behaviour), and a pure ufSliceTargets maps unfold's selection shape (card/group sel · wire · none) to the slice targets — acceptance-provable red-then-green. The style port records a CLASH instead of working around it (ruled): the legacy THEMES system styles only the canvas surface via --* vars which unfold does not consume (unfold has its own --uf-* palette and light/dark toggle), so the style tab ports FONT (FONTS stays the single source; theming.applyFont additionally sets --uf-font, consumed by unfold's CSS so the choice reaches the primary surface) and hosts unfold's light/dark appearance control — the THEME CHIPS STAY LEGACY-ONLY pending a design ruling on mapping THEMES to unfold's palette. Assumptions recorded (amend if Chris rules otherwise): (1) row assignment 3+2 as above; (2) the slice tab follows the legacy pane's contract — no selection = the full diagram text (enables whole-doc copy), a selected wire slices around both endpoints, sel wins over wire; (3) the slice tab refreshes on selection commit and on tab activation, not per-keystroke; (4) the style tab's light/dark control drives the same applyDark path as the existing ufTheme button (which remains in the floating toolbar); (5) --uf-font defaults to unfold's current stack when no font pref is set, and applyFont keeps writing --node-font so legacy is unchanged. Execution order: this plan lands BEFORE m5-a-verbs (that plan's initUnfold signature is cumulative on this one). Resume: npm run novakai:status -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m5-p-tabs2.plan.json · prove behaviour: npm run novakai:acceptance -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m5-p-tabs2.plan.json · runtime criteria (browser, 0-context verify): (1) the dock strip shows two rows — reveal · io · mermaid, then slice · style — with the collapse chevron still at the top right; (2) with nothing selected the slice tab shows the full diagram .mmd and a node count; selecting a card refreshes it to that node's neighbourhood slice with boundary-stub count; copy fills the clipboard; (3) the legacy slice pane behaves exactly as before (same text for the same selection — one code path); (4) the style tab changes the font and unfold's text visibly changes; reload restores it; the legacy canvas font also follows (one FONTS source); (5) the style tab toggles unfold light/dark identically to the toolbar button; (6) the io/mermaid/reveal tabs are byte-for-byte unaffected; dock persistence (unfold.dock) restores the new tabs after reload; (7) zero console errors through boot → all five tabs → slice copy → font change → reload.

phases.title: Pure slice targeting + one slice path
phases.subtitle: ufSliceTargets (acceptance red first) and SliceApi.sliceFor — the single serialisation path both surfaces trigger

phases.title: Font reaches unfold
phases.subtitle: theming.applyFont additionally sets --uf-font; FONTS stays the single source; legacy untouched

phases.title: Two-row strip + slice/style tabs
phases.subtitle: the dock strip stacks two rows; slice and style tab bodies land; deps widen { slice, theming }

intent.problem: The legacy slice pane reads ctx.state.sel (a multi-select Set). Unfold's selection is a different shape — a single card/group id (spec.sel) or a selected wire (two endpoint ids) or nothing — and 'which ids feed the slice' is a real decision that would otherwise land as unprovable DOM-bound branching inside the tab painter.
intent.approach: One pure function ufSliceTargets(sel, wire) → string[] in a dependency-free file src/panel/unfold-slice.ts (ufDockReduce/ufEscAction precedent). Rules: (a) a non-empty sel wins → [sel]; (b) else a wire → its two endpoints, deduplicated for a self-loop; (c) else → [] meaning 'full diagram' (the legacy pane's no-selection contract, which enables whole-doc copy). An empty-string sel counts as no selection.
intent.rationale: E2/H1 factor-to-pure: the slice tab's load-bearing claim — what you have selected is exactly what gets sliced — becomes red-then-green acceptance cases instead of prose.

fm.name: ufSliceTargets
fm.description: pure mapping from unfold's selection shape to slice target ids: a non-empty selected card/group id wins → [sel]; else a selected wire → its two endpoints deduplicated for a self-loop; else [] meaning full diagram (the legacy no-selection contract); an empty-string sel counts as no selection

interfaces.name: ufSliceTargets
accepts: wire: { a: string; b: string } | null

cases.name: a selected card slices around that card
no selection at all means the full diagram (empty targets)
a selected wire slices around both endpoints
card selection wins over a stale wire
a self-loop wire dedupes to one target
an empty-string sel counts as no selection (wire still slices)

intent.problem: The slice serialisation (sliceIds union → sliceStubs → mermaid.toMermaid({only}) → info label) lives inline in the legacy pane's render(). The unfold slice tab cannot reach it without duplicating the computation — the exact drift novakai exists to kill.
intent.approach: Factor the computation into sliceFor(ids: string[]) → { text, info } exposed on SliceApi: empty ids → full-diagram text + node-count info; otherwise the union slice with boundary stubs and the existing label wording. The legacy render() delegates to sliceFor([...state.sel]) — behaviour unchanged.
intent.rationale: One code path per verb regardless of surface (the files.loadMmdText precedent from P-panel): the legacy pane and the unfold tab are two triggers of the same proven behaviour.

fm.name: initSlice
fm.description: build the legacy slice pane chrome and expose the one slice-serialisation path: sliceFor(ids) computes the neighbourhood slice text + info label (empty ids = full diagram), and the legacy pane's render() delegates to it with the editor selection

intent.problem: SliceApi's public surface is render alone — the unfold slice tab has no typed door to the serialisation.
intent.approach: Widen the interface: render() stays; sliceFor(ids: string[]): { text: string; info: string } joins it.
intent.rationale: The composition root passes slice into unfold's deps; the type is the contract the map gates.

intent.problem: applyFont writes --node-font, which unfold's CSS never reads — a font chosen in the style tab would change the invisible legacy canvas and not the surface in front of the user (the recorded THEMES/--uf-* clash, first instance).
intent.approach: applyFont additionally sets --uf-font on the document root; unfold's CSS consumes var(--uf-font, <current stack>) so the default look is unchanged until a font is chosen. --node-font keeps being written — legacy is untouched, FONTS stays the single source of font definitions.
intent.rationale: The ruled boundary: font genuinely IS surface-independent (one FONTS table), so it ports; the THEMES palette is not (it targets legacy vars only), so the chips stay legacy-only pending a ruling instead of a workaround.

fm.name: applyFont
fm.description: apply a font choice app-wide from the single FONTS source: sets --node-font (legacy canvas) and --uf-font (the primary surface consumes it with the current stack as fallback), persists the pref

intent.problem: The dock strip is one row of three tabs; slice and style have no home, and a single row grows stupidly long as tabs accumulate (Chris's ruling).
intent.approach: DOCK_TABS grows to ['reveal','io','mermaid','slice','style'] — the reducer is tab-agnostic by design and needs NO change; persisted dock state with the new tabs normalizes through the existing load action. The strip stacks TWO ROWS (row 1: reveal · io · mermaid; row 2: slice · style) with the collapse chevron staying at the top right. SLICE tab body: info line + readonly textarea + copy button, fed by deps.slice.sliceFor(ufSliceTargets(spec.sel, <selected wire endpoints>)), refreshed on selection commit and on tab activation. STYLE tab body (appearance): a light/dark control driving the same applyDark path as the ufTheme toolbar button, and a font select built from FONT_ORDER/FONTS wired to deps.theming.applyFont. deps widen to { selection, camera, files, mermaid, slice, theming } — main.ts already builds slice and theming before unfold.
intent.rationale: The dock stays a dumb painter of ufDockReduce (unchanged, already acceptance-proven); the new bodies trigger the modules' single code paths (sliceFor, applyFont) so no behaviour is duplicated across surfaces; the two-row strip is the ruled scalability fix.

fm.name: initUnfold
fm.description: build the primary surface (own DOM + own CSS, the planner isolation pattern) bound to ctx; reads ctx.state + ctx.bodies, writes only through the shared hooks path; the dock carries a temporary legacy-compare affordance instead of a ✕ (M4 correction); the panel is a real dock driven by the pure ufDockReduce — five tabs on a two-row strip (reveal · io · mermaid / slice · style) anchored at the reveal strip, chevron collapse to a slim rail, left-border drag resize, state persisted under unfold.dock; the slice tab serialises through SliceApi.sliceFor on ufSliceTargets, the style tab drives appearance (light/dark + font via theming.applyFont)

interfaces.name: initUnfold
accepts: deps: { selection: SelectionApi; camera: CameraApi; files: FilesApi; mermaid: MermaidApi; slice: SliceApi; theming: ThemingApi }
